messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "setup-project",
      "title": "Initialize project structure",
      "description": "Create project folder, initialize npm (package.json), add .gitignore, and create src and public directories to host server and frontend files.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder exists with package.json, .gitignore, and src/ and public/ directories created."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install required dependencies and dev tools",
      "description": "Install Express and development tools (e.g., nodemon). Install Tailwind CSS, postcss, and autoprefixer as devDependencies and add npm scripts for building Tailwind.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "npm install completed, dependencies listed in package.json, and scripts for dev and build present (e.g., start, dev, build:css)."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind and build pipeline",
      "description": "Initialize tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, create an input CSS file with Tailwind directives, and verify build outputs a usable CSS file in public/ (or served location).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist and running the build script generates a CSS file containing Tailwind utilities in the public folder."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Create Express server to serve static frontend",
      "description": "Implement server (e.g., src/server.js) that serves files from public/ and listens on a configurable port; add start and dev scripts to package.json.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts, serves static files from public/, and returns index.html on root path when visiting http://localhost:<port>."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-frontend-page",
      "title": "Build basic frontend with Hello World text",
      "description": "Add public/index.html that includes the built Tailwind CSS and contains a visible \"hello world\" text element with an identifiable id or class for dynamic styling.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "index.html renders the hello world text styled by Tailwind and is accessible via the Express server in a browser."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-ui-controls",
      "title": "Add frontend UI controls to change text color",
      "description": "Implement color controls (e.g., color input, preset buttons, or Tailwind class toggles) and JavaScript that updates the hello world text color in real time. Ensure controls are accessible and responsive.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-frontend-page"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "User can change the hello world text color via the UI and see the color update immediately in the browser without a full page reload."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test functionality and add README",
      "description": "Manually test the flow: start server, open page, change colors. Document setup, dev and build commands, and how to extend or persist color selections in README.md.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-ui-controls"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README contains clear run/build instructions and tests confirm the color controls work across common browsers and the server runs as documented."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: start with project scaffolding, then install dependencies, configure Tailwind build, create server, build the static page, add interactive UI controls to change text color, and finish with testing and documentation. Dependencies follow the natural build order to avoid circular references."
}
